proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65; the candidate age variable) are associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (race/ethnicity: white/black/hispanic/asian/other).
Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_045 (insurance: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: OLS slope of pfs_months on feature_078 = +0.1740 mo per unit (p=0.00e+00). Spearman r reaches +0.85; mean pfs_months rises monotonically from 0.85 mo in the lowest decile of feature_078 to 6.86 mo in the highest decile.
One-way ANOVA across the 5 race categories: F-test p=0.689. Group means range only 3.74-3.80 mo; no overall difference detected.
One-way ANOVA across the 4 insurance categories: F-test p=0.794. Group means range 3.73-3.75 mo; no overall difference detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2, candidate stage or risk group) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotonically decreasing pattern.
Higher feature_013 (continuous, range 0.1-3622, candidate PSA-like biomarker) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with feature_051=1 (binary, candidate metastasis/disease-burden flag) have shorter pfs_months than feature_051=0.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman r=-0.386, p=0.00e+00. Means: feature_057=0 -> 4.68 mo (n=17592), =1 -> 3.49 mo (n=24971), =2 -> 2.38 mo (n=7437). Strong monotone decrease.
Spearman r=-0.223, p=0.00e+00. Higher feature_013 values associate with shorter PFS.
Mean pfs_months: feature_051=0 -> 4.031 (n=22519) vs feature_051=1 -> 3.509 (n=27481). Difference (1 - 0) = -0.521 mo, t-test p=9.83e-182.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8; resembles serum albumin) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Patients with feature_109=1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have different pfs_months than feature_109=0.
Patients with feature_039=1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have different pfs_months than feature_039=0.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman r=-0.1344, p=3.72e-200. Significant negative association: higher feature_006 -> shorter PFS.
Spearman r=0.1180, p=1.40e-154. Significant positive association: higher feature_009 -> longer PFS.
feature_109=1 vs 0: mean diff = +0.100 mo (n1=4996, n0=45004), t-test p=0.0009. Modestly higher PFS in feature_109=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable OLS, feature_078 retains a positive effect, feature_057 retains a negative effect, log(feature_013) retains a negative effect, feature_051 retains a negative effect, feature_009 retains a positive effect, and feature_006 retains a negative effect on pfs_months.

After adjusting for clinical covariates (age, stage, log-PSA, feature_051, feature_006, feature_009), race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) coefficients remain non-significant — i.e., observed PFS does not differ by demographics conditional on clinical features.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + np.log1p(feature_013) + feature_051 + feature_006 + feature_009 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable OLS (n=50000, R^2=0.969). Coefficients (per unit, mo): feature_078=+0.1737 (p=0.0e+00); C(feature_057)[T.1]=-1.178 (p=0.0e+00); C(feature_057)[T.2]=-2.286 (p=0.0e+00); log1p(feature_013)=-0.383 (p=0.0e+00); feature_051=0.001 (p=8.7e-01); feature_006=-0.0768 (p=0.0e+00); feature_009=+0.478 (p=0.0e+00). All clinical predictors retain expected directions and remain highly significant.

analyses.code: Same multivariable model; inspect race/insurance coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (vs reference): black=+0.0022 (p=0.78), hispanic=+0.0017 (p=0.83), asian (reference: white) — all non-significant. Adjusted insurance coefficients all p>0.9. Demographics add no incremental information after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months for black patients (feature_018=black) differs from white patients (reference).
Mean pfs_months for hispanic patients differs from white patients.
Mean pfs_months for asian patients differs from white patients.
Mean pfs_months for medicaid-insured patients differs from privately-insured patients (reference).
Mean pfs_months for uninsured patients differs from privately-insured patients.
Mean pfs_months for medicare patients differs from privately-insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: black vs white: diff=-0.0005 mo, p=0.987 (n_black=6064, n_white=32378).
hispanic vs white: diff=-0.0037 mo, p=0.886.
asian vs white: diff=+0.0561 mo, p=0.145. Direction is nominally positive (~0.06 mo) but not significant.
medicaid vs private: diff=+0.0046 mo, p=0.871.
uninsured vs private: diff=-0.0101 mo, p=0.832.
medicare vs private: diff=+0.0183 mo, p=0.362.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (race × feature_051 interaction).
The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (race × feature_109 interaction).
The effect of feature_039 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (race × feature_039 interaction).
The effect of feature_043 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (race × feature_043 interaction).
The effect of feature_035 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (race × feature_035 interaction).

analyses.code: OLS interaction model with joint F-test
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test for race × feature_051 interaction (4 df): F=0.620, p=0.649. No heterogeneity detected.
Joint F-test race × feature_109: F=1.414, p=0.226.
Joint F-test race × feature_039: F=0.250, p=0.910.
Joint F-test race × feature_043: F=0.187, p=0.945.
Joint F-test race × feature_035: F=1.233, p=0.294.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across insurance categories (insurance × feature_051 interaction).
The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs across insurance categories (insurance × feature_109 interaction).
The effect of feature_039 on pfs_months differs across insurance categories (insurance × feature_039 interaction).
The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months grows in magnitude with higher feature_078 (age × feature_051 interaction; coefficient negative).
The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months varies with feature_078 (age × feature_109 interaction).
The effect of feature_039 on pfs_months varies with feature_078 (age × feature_039 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.00416 mo per (year × feature_051), p=1.67e-12. Significant: each unit of feature_078 amplifies the negative feature_051 effect.
Interaction coef = -0.00055, p=0.600. Not significant.
Interaction coef = -0.00080, p=0.440. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across feature_057 strata (stage × feature_051 interaction).
The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs across feature_057 strata.
The effect of feature_039 on pfs_months differs across feature_057 strata.

analyses.result_summary: Joint F=5.00, p=0.0067. Significant interaction; the gap between feature_051 strata varies somewhat with stage (smallest in stage 2 where overall PFS is shortest).
Joint F=2.24, p=0.107. Not significant.
Joint F=0.14, p=0.870. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age (feature_078) and stage (feature_057), feature_051 retains a strong negative effect on pfs_months.
After adjusting for age and stage, feature_109 retains a positive effect on pfs_months.
After adjusting for age and stage, feature_039 retains a positive effect on pfs_months.
After adjusting for age and stage, no other binary feature has an effect on pfs_months larger in magnitude than ±0.05 months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) for each binary col, ranked by p
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 adjusted coef = -0.5197 mo, p=0.00e+00 (top of screen).
feature_109 adjusted coef = +0.1497 mo, p=1.54e-46 (rank 2 in screen).
feature_039 adjusted coef = +0.1421 mo, p=8.45e-43 (rank 3 in screen).

Aside from feature_051, feature_109, feature_039, the remaining binary features all have |adjusted coefficient| <= 0.04 mo. Notable significant but tiny effects: feature_027 (+0.020 mo, p=1.5e-3), feature_043 (-0.023, p=3.3e-3), feature_079 (-0.033, p=0.04), feature_035 (-0.016, p=0.04). No additional clinically meaningful signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months decreases monotonically across rising quartiles of feature_013 (PSA-like), with the highest quartile having the shortest mean PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_013 quartile: q0=4.311, q1=3.933, q2=3.618, q3=3.115. Linear trend coef = -0.390 mo per quartile, p=0.00e+00. Confirms a stepwise dose-response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of log(feature_013) on pfs_months differs across feature_057 strata (PSA × stage interaction).
Within feature_057=0 (stage 0), higher feature_013 still associates with shorter pfs_months.
Within feature_057=2 (stage 2), higher feature_013 still associates with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test for PSA × stage: F=30.55, p=5.52e-14 (df=2). Significant interaction.
Within stage 0: spearman r=-0.251, p=5.63e-250 (n=17592).
Within stage 2: spearman r=-0.242, p=1.11e-99 (n=7437). Effect persists.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within white patients.
The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within black patients.
The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within hispanic patients.
The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within asian patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within white: feature_051 mean diff = -0.5254 mo, p=3.77e-120 (n0=14563, n1=17815).
Within black: feature_051 mean diff = -0.5058 mo, p=2.19e-22 (n0=2703, n1=3361).
Within hispanic: feature_051 mean diff = -0.5291 mo, p=6.22e-30.
Within asian: feature_051 mean diff = -0.5353 mo, p=3.66e-13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within privately-insured patients.
The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within medicare patients.
The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within medicaid patients.
The negative feature_051 effect on pfs_months is observed within uninsured patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within private: f051 diff=-0.5064 mo, p=5.34e-69.
Within medicare: f051 diff=-0.5221 mo, p=1.79e-78.
Within medicaid: f051 diff=-0.5364 mo, p=1.20e-28.
Within uninsured: f051 diff=-0.6009 mo, p=3.05e-11 (largest magnitude across insurance strata).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months varies with feature_009 (continuous albumin-like biomarker).
The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months varies with feature_006.
The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months varies with feature_094.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=-0.00031, p=0.977.
Interaction coef=+0.00148, p=0.273.
Interaction coef=-0.00045, p=0.035. Marginally significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 (n≈500) have substantially longer pfs_months than patients with neither, beyond what each individual main effect would predict (super-additive effect).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ C(_both_pf) + age + stage where _both_pf = feature_109+feature_039
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted PFS contrast: 1-of-2 positive (only one of the two flags) vs 0-of-2: -0.0040 mo, p=0.62. 2-of-2 (both) vs 0-of-2: +1.4218 mo, p=0.00e+00. The both-positive group shows roughly 5x larger benefit than the sum of single-positive effects (~0.30 mo expected vs +1.42 mo observed) — strong synergistic interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 differs across racial categories (age × race interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint F=0.78 (df=4), p=0.536. No heterogeneity of age effect by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on log(feature_013) differs across racial categories (PSA × race interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint F=1.14 (df=4), p=0.336. No racial heterogeneity in the PSA effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (stage × race interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint F=0.51 (df=8), p=0.853. No stage × race interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 on pfs_months differs across insurance categories (stage × insurance interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint F=0.92 (df=6), p=0.482. No stage × insurance interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each feature_057 (stage) stratum, higher feature_006 is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within stage 0: r=-0.143 (n=17592, p=7.56e-81); stage 1: r=-0.153 (n=24971, p=9.14e-131); stage 2: r=-0.131 (n=7437, p=8.96e-30). Effect direction is consistently negative across all strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Prevalence of feature_051=1 differs across racial categories (i.e., disease-burden flag is unequally distributed across races).
Prevalence of feature_109=1 differs across racial categories.
Distribution of feature_057 levels differs across racial categories.

analyses.result_summary: chi2 p=0.818. Prevalence range across races: 0.544–0.554. No significant racial disparity.
chi2 p=0.434. Prevalence range: 0.094–0.107. No racial disparity.
chi2 p=0.843. Stage distribution does not differ by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Prevalence of feature_051=1 differs across insurance categories.
Prevalence of feature_109=1 differs across insurance categories.
Prevalence of feature_043=1 differs across insurance categories.

analyses.result_summary: chi2 p=0.372. Prevalence range 0.546–0.562.
chi2 p=0.198. Range 0.197–0.216; uninsured slightly elevated but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean feature_078 (age) differs across racial categories — i.e., demographic groups have different age structures that could confound the age-PFS effect.
Mean feature_078 (age) differs across insurance categories — uninsured patients are notably younger than medicare patients.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA p=0.674. Mean ages range 64.96–65.23 years across races; differences are negligible.

ANOVA p=0.881. Mean ages range 64.82–65.00 years across insurance groups; uninsured age is virtually identical to medicare age (~65 yrs), inconsistent with the conventional pattern of younger uninsured.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A single multivariable OLS containing feature_078, C(feature_057), log(feature_013), feature_051, feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, feature_039, race, insurance, plus the feature_078:feature_051 and C(feature_057):feature_051 interactions explains nearly all of the variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.95).
Within this final model, the C(feature_057):feature_051 interaction is positive — meaning the negative effect of feature_051 attenuates (becomes less negative) at higher feature_057 stages.
Within this final model, the feature_078:feature_051 interaction is negative — meaning the negative effect of feature_051 grows in magnitude with increasing age.

analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.9703, R^2_adj = 0.9703, n=50000. The clinical features (feature_078, feature_057, log feature_013, feature_051, feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, feature_039) plus their feature_051 interactions explain >97% of pfs_months variance.
C(feature_057)[T.1]:feature_051 = +0.0151 mo (p=2.89e-02); C(feature_057)[T.2]:feature_051 = +0.0573 mo (p=3.57e-09). Both positive — feature_051 penalty is less severe at higher stages.
feature_078:feature_051 coef = -0.00453 mo per (year × feature_051), p=1.06e-46. Negative as predicted: each year of age intensifies the feature_051 detriment.
